definitions.definition: A person or a person's legal representative who hires or avails of any services for a consideration with the intention of availing such service for a purpose that is allowed by law.
A movement that aims to protect consumers from the exploitation by sellers.
A legislation that was enacted to provide better protection of the interests of consumers.
The rights that a consumer is entitled to, which include the right to be protected against the marketing of goods and services which are hazardous to life and property.
The right to be protected against the marketing of goods and services which are hazardous to life and property.
The right to be informed about the quality, quantity, potency, purity, standard and price of goods or services so as to protect the consumer's interest.
The right to choose between the products of competing manufacturers and sellers and to be informed about the quality, quantity, potency, purity, standard and price of goods or services.
The right to be heard and to be assured that consumers' grievances shall be given their due consideration and to be dealt with in a fair and speedy manner.
A forum that is established to provide speedy and simple redressal of consumer disputes.
A body that is formed at the state level to hear appeals against the orders of the District Commission.
The apex consumer protection body in India that deals with cases where the value of the commodity or service is above Rs. 1 crore.
A body that is formed at the district level to hear consumer disputes.
A process in which a neutral third party assists the parties to a dispute in reaching a mutually acceptable settlement.
A process in which a neutral third party makes a binding decision on the dispute.
A court that is established to provide speedy and simple redressal of consumer disputes.
Goods which do not conform to the express warranty or implied warranty.

Any trade practice which, for the purpose of promoting the sale, use or supply of any goods or service, is likely to mislead the consumer as to the price at which the goods or service has been obtained or is to be obtained, or as to the service or quality of the goods or service.
The protection of consumers from exploitation and ensuring that they receive fair treatment.
A trade practice that is fair and honest and does not mislead or deceive the consumer.
